prediction.summary: A critical data exfiltration incident has been detected involving the 'svc_etl' account from host 10.20.3.15. Over 1.1GB of sensitive 'FINANCE_PII' data, specifically '/data/finance_q2.zip', was uploaded via 'curl' to a suspicious external domain, 'upload.evil-cdn.net' (185.220.101.44). Multiple security systems, including DLP, firewall, SIEM, and IDS, flagged this activity as a MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel.

timeline.event: Sensitive label 'FINANCE_PII' detected in outbound transfer by 'svc_etl'.
Data exfiltration pattern identified: large encrypted upload to rare external domain.
MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel suspected from host 10.20.3.15.
Volume spike (14x baseline) detected from 10.20.3.15 to 185.220.101.44:443.
CRITICAL alert for data exfiltration by user 'svc_etl' to 'upload.evil-cdn.net'.

recommendations: Immediately isolate host 10.20.3.15 from the network to prevent further data loss or lateral movement.
Block all outbound connections to 'upload.evil-cdn.net' (185.220.101.44) at the firewall and proxy.
Investigate the 'svc_etl' account for compromise, including password reset, review of recent activity, and MFA logs.
Conduct a full forensic analysis of host 10.20.3.15 to determine the initial compromise vector, persistence mechanisms, and any other malicious activity.
Assess the full scope of data exfiltrated and notify relevant stakeholders (e.g., legal, privacy, management).
